page: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-16 is an e2 family virtual machine configured with 16 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The instance is powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. This CPU configuration includes 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating with dedicated CPU allocation. The processor features a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. It supports AVX, AES, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instruction sets, though it lacks AVX-512 and AMX support. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates that the single-core CPU performance of the AMD EPYC 7B12 processor on this instance is strong, placing in the top 25% of tested systems via stress-ng. However, multi-core CPU performance and overall system benchmarks generally fall into the average tier, representing the middle 50% of results. This average performance profile is consistent across Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, Passmark CPU and memory scores, and static web serving throughput. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also register in the average category.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio, the instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require specialized hardware accelerators or local NVMe storage. The average performance across database operations, Redis SET operations, and software compilation suggests a balanced profile for memory-bound applications. Qualitatively, the e2-highmem-16 offers a resource-dense configuration for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity without the premium associated with higher-tier compute or storage-optimized instances, though the lack of local storage and GPU acceleration limits its utility for large-scale AI training or high-IOPS database tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-16 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor with 8 physical cores and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, placing in the top 25% of tested systems, while multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently rank in the average tier. The instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as Redis caching, software compilation, and moderate database operations that benefit from a high memory footprint but do not require local NVMe storage or specialized hardware accelerators.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-16 is an x86_64 instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B12 CPU. It offers strong single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-CPU ratio and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
